name: Chucho Valdés
abstract: Dionisio Jesús Valdés Rodríguez, better known as Chucho (born October 9, 1941), is a Cuban pianist, bandleader, composer and arranger whose career spans over 50 years. An original member of the Orquesta Cubana de Música Moderna, in 1973 he founded the group Irakere, one of Cuba's best-known Latin jazz bands.

about_quotes.speaker: Luis Conte
about_quotes.evidence: Chucho goes, “Hey when there’s a break coming up, you know, I’ll give you – just keep your eyes open you know.” ... So I go up there and I started playing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Luis Conte interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 24, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Paquito D'Rivera
about_quotes.evidence: I was the first call together with Chucho and Leo Brouwer... I was among the founding members of that orchestra
about_quotes.doc_title: Paquito D'Rivera — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve been to Cuba and I’ve played with Irakere and Chucho Valdez and some of the great musicians down there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Freeman interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Palmieri
about_quotes.evidence: my friend Manny Lopez was traveling with the Irakere, and he brought them to the studio when I was recording for CBS. They heard...Chucho Valdés heard Lucumi Macumba Voodoo.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Palmieri — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wilson "Chembo" Corniel
about_quotes.evidence: I was nominated along with Chucho Valdes... But when you have a name like Chucho Valdes, “Who is Chembo?” “Who is Chembo?”
about_quotes.doc_title: Wilson "Chembo" Corniel interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, November 15, 2019
